free_text: Activation cross sections with 14 MeV neutrons
accelerated deuteron to 200 keV
The neutron yield was 10**10 to 10**11 n/sec
The samples, either in foil or in powder form, were of
spectroscopically pure quality except in the case of
Na and Al.
The compound of Na used was of E.Merck guaranteed
reagent quality.
For Al samples chemically pure aluminum foils were
used.
Foil samples in the form of disks of 1 inch dia. and
powder samples pressed in a perspex sample pan of
1 inch dia. were sandwiched between two reference
foils (in this case they were Cu or Al disks of
1 inch dia. of 1 mil thick)
End window beta counter
For monitoring of neutron yield
The authors do not say which reaction was measured
against which of the two monitors.
Usual corrections such as geometry, back scattering,
source absorption, window and air absorption were
applied to determine the absolute beta disintegration
rate including the probability of K-capture for
beta activity.
Correction due to source absorption was calculated
according to the following formula
F(S) = (1-exp(-mu*T))/mu T
where   F(S) = source absorption correction factor
mu   = mass absorption coefficient
in cm2/mg
T    = sample thickness in mg/cm2
For beta energies less than 1 MeV, the corrections
due to absorption in the window were taken into
account.
At beta energies of the order of 1 MeV the window
correction becomes negligible as it is less than 1%
and need not be taken into account.
Cross sections given are averages of at
least 3 experimental runs, and errors given are the
deviations from the average value.
